comments.text: The following was suggested by ChaptGPT 5.2 Pro. Both Gemini 3 Pro and Claude 4.5 Sonnet say that it is correct: https://chatgpt.com/s/t_696d3f4ce56c81918a918ff6a87eae54

ChatGPT 5.2 Pro locates multiple issues with the claimed proof, including that the final answer contradicts known bounds, and that the key claim in Step 4 is both unjustified and false in general. This section of the wiki for AI contributions to Erdos problems is also of relevance.

As per Tao, $t(r) = 2$ for $r \ge 6$ isn’t possible because it’s inconsistent with the [EHT91] result above. Some tips: ask GPT-5.2 Pro to check its own work. Currently it’s mathematically the strongest model and can actually outperform “independent” checks by Gemini/Claude.

I want to second this. Slightly reformulating the proof, starting a new chat, and the chance is high that GPT 5.2 finds something. It may even be enough to stay in the same chat and write something like "I have serious doubts. Look again with hawk eyes." Or "My Professor is not convinced. Where might she see problems or holes?"

Actually straightforward is enough for current models: “Can you check this proof again for correctness?”. Probably also better because e.g. the professor one the model will focus on issues that occur in professor-student relationships (which may not include research-level issues).
